title: Investors Debate Which Stock Will Create the Most Millionaires in the Next 10 Years. 'Not the Safest. Not Your Favorite'
detail:
Investors Debate Which Stock Will Create the Most Millionaires in the Next 10 Years. 'Not the Safest. Not Your Favorite'
Investors Debate Which Stock Will Create the Most Millionaires in the Next 10 Years. 'Not the Safest. Not Your Favorite'
Adrian Volenik
Mon, July 13, 2026 at 7:01 PM EDT 7 min read
RKLB
RDDT
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Benzinga and Yahoo Finance LLC may earn commission or revenue on some items through the links below.

If you could only pick one stock to hold for the next decade, which would it be? Just the one you honestly believe has the best chance of turning ordinary investors into millionaires.

That was the question posed in a recent Reddit discussion, and it sparked hundreds of responses from investors sharing their highest-conviction ideas. The original poster added two important conditions: "Not the safest. Not your favorite." 

The focus wasn't on steady blue-chip stocks or safe index funds. It was on companies that could potentially deliver life-changing returns if everything goes right.

Don't Miss:

Deloitte's #1 Fastest-Growing Software Company Lets Users Earn Money Just by Scrolling — Investors Can Still Get In at $0.52/Share

If there was a new fund backed by Jeff Bezos offering a 7-9% target yield with monthly dividends would you invest in it?

Space, Satellites and Big Bets

One of the most frequently mentioned stocks was Rocket Lab (NASDAQ:RKLB). Supporters pointed to the company's leadership, execution and long-term position in the growing space industry.

"Rocket Lab is run so exceptionally well that I truly believe anyone with patience over the next decade will be rewarded massively," one investor wrote.

Others said they continue buying shares during pullbacks because they view Rocket Lab as a long-term winner, not a short-term trade.

AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ:ASTS) was another favorite. The company is building satellite technology designed to connect directly to ordinary smartphones. One commenter said it was the stock in their portfolio most likely to generate "f***-you money" if the company's vision becomes reality.

A lot of investors think the space industry today is kind of like where artificial intelligence was a few years ago–risky, hard to predict, but with the chance to grow in a big way.

Trending: What If Your Budget Adjusted To Your Life—Instead Of The Other Way Around?

AI, Quantum Computing and Energy

Artificial intelligence was another major theme throughout the discussion.

Nebius Group (NASDAQ:NBIS) received repeated mentions from investors looking beyond the biggest AI names. Supporters argued that growing demand for computing power, data centers and electricity could create opportunities throughout the AI supply chain.

Some investors are also looking beyond public markets for companies tied to the future of AI and computing. Immersed, the creator of the top-ranked productivity app on Meta Quest, has attracted attention from investors betting that more work will eventually move into virtual and AI-powered environments. The platform has been used by more than 1.5 million people and has attracted more than 75,000 people to the waitlist for its Visor headset. Pre-IPO shares are currently available for $0.79 each, with eligible investors able to receive up to 20% bonus shares.

title: Money Turning Into More Money Sounds Too Good to Be True. Does Building a Large Portfolio Actually Require Less Work Than People Assume?
detail:
Money Turning Into More Money Sounds Too Good to Be True. Does Building a Large Portfolio Actually Require Less Work Than People Assume?
Money Turning Into More Money Sounds Too Good to Be True. Does Building a Large Portfolio Actually Require Less Work Than People Assume?
Adrian Volenik
Sat, July 11, 2026 at 9:01 AM EDT 7 min read
RDDT
Trade Reddit on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Benzinga and Yahoo Finance LLC may earn commission or revenue on some items through the links below.

One nonprofit executive who grew up in poverty described a surprising realization after spending hours reading about investing, retirement accounts and other financial strategies.

"What bugged me out was realizing that this stuff isn't like, super unattainable," he wrote on Reddit's r/personalfinance forum recently. "That a large investment portfolio really is just…actively investing. It doesn't take a ton of work."

For years, he assumed serious wealth-building was reserved for doctors, lawyers, tech founders and other high earners. Like many people who grew up struggling financially, he viewed large investment portfolios, charitable funds and advanced financial planning as tools for an entirely different class of people. Then he started doing the math and realized many of those opportunities were far more accessible than he had imagined.

Don't Miss:

1.5M+ Users. $29M Raised. Shares Still at $0.79 — Learn How to Invest Before the Deadline

The AI Gold Rush Needs Picks And Shovels Too — This Company Is Building The Infrastructure Layer.

The Secret Isn't a Secret

The post quickly filled with responses from people who had experienced the same thing.

Many described growing up in low-income households and carrying those financial habits long after their incomes increased. One commenter said she still automatically thinks first-class airline tickets are "for rich people" even though she can afford them. Another admitted that every major household repair still feels like a crisis despite having significant savings.

A lot of commenters once assumed building wealth required extraordinary income, insider knowledge or complicated investment strategies. Instead, they discovered that the foundation of wealth-building is often surprisingly simple.

One person described successful investing as "boring consistency spread over many years."

Trending: Wall Street Traders Pay Thousands For Market Data. This Platform Gives Everyday Investors Access To Advanced Tools.

Several people highlighted retirement accounts, Roth IRAs, employer-sponsored plans and low-cost index funds as the primary tools responsible for much of their financial progress. Others said they were surprised to learn that concepts such as backdoor Roth contributions, compound growth and long-term investing were not reserved for the ultra-wealthy.

The discussion also pointed out something important: while a lot of people grow their money by investing in the overall market, it's now easier than ever to find different ways to build wealth. These days, you can stick with things like index funds and retirement accounts, or you can look into startups and newer companies that used to be off-limits unless you were really wealthy or well-connected.

Story Continues

title: RDDT Stock Gains After-Hours — Why This Analyst Says Reddit's Human-Powered Content Could Be Its Biggest Advantage In The AI Era
detail:
RDDT Stock Gains After-Hours — Why This Analyst Says Reddit's Human-Powered Content Could Be Its Biggest Advantage In The AI Era
RDDT Stock Gains After-Hours — Why This Analyst Says Reddit's Human-Powered Content Could Be Its Biggest Advantage In The AI Era · Stocktwits
Aveek Bhowmik
Fri, July 10, 2026 at 7:29 PM EDT 2 min read
RDDT
Trade Reddit on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Needham maintained its 'Buy' rating on Reddit and said the company's human-generated discussions give it an edge.

Growing adoption of AI chatbots could increase demand for authentic, human-produced content, according to Needham.

Wall Street remains broadly bullish on Reddit, with 21 of 32 analysts rating the stock 'Buy' or 'Strong Buy,' according to Koyfin.

Reddit (RDDT) could have further upside as the rapid adoption of artificial intelligence makes authentic, human-generated content more valuable, according to Needham. The brokerage maintained its 'Buy' rating on the social media platform and reiterated a $300 price target, implying nearly 50% upside from Friday's closing price.

Shares of Reddit gained 0.5% after-hours on Friday, after ending the regular session with a loss of 2.48%.

See what 10M+ investors are talking about. Get the Stocktwits Daily Rip for what retail is watching right now, free to your inbox

Human Content Could Become Reddit's Biggest Strength

Needham said Reddit's vast repository of human conversations gives it a competitive advantage as AI-generated content becomes more widespread.

"RDDT's core asset becomes more valuable over time, because it is 100% human dialog and information, at scale, which is a competitive advantage in an environment of proliferating algorithms," said the analyst in a note to clients, according to CNBC.

The analyst added that Reddit provides "a human layer of authentication and advice," which many consumers seek before acting on recommendations generated by large language models or algorithms.

AI Adoption Could Increase Demand For Reddit

The use of generative AI tools such as ChatGPT has surged in recent years. According to the Pew Research Center, around half of U.S. adults now report using AI chatbots, a significant increase from 2024. The survey also found that about one in four respondents use generative AI tools every day.

As AI-generated content becomes more prevalent, Needham believes authentic, human-produced discussions could become harder to find, making Reddit a more valuable destination for users seeking non-AI insights.

RDDT Stock: What Stocktwits Retail Sentiment Says

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment for RDDT was 'bearish,' while message volume was 'normal' at the time of writing. Over the past seven days, message volume around the stock has surged by 133%, while its retail watcher base has risen 0.1% in the same period.

Needham's positive stance aligns with broader Wall Street sentiment. According to Koyfin data, of the 32 analysts covering Reddit, 21 have a 'Buy' or 'Strong Buy' rating on the stock, while 10 have a 'Hold' rating. The 12-month average target on the stock is $225.92, representing a potential upside of nearly 13% from the last close.

title: Reddit Inc. (RDDT) Stock Sinks As Market Gains: Here's Why
detail:
Reddit Inc. (RDDT) Stock Sinks As Market Gains: Here's Why
Reddit Inc. (RDDT) Stock Sinks As Market Gains: Here's Why · Zacks
Zacks Equity Research
Fri, July 10, 2026 at 5:45 PM EDT 3 min read
RDDT
^GSPC
^DJI
Trade Reddit on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Reddit Inc. (RDDT) closed at $195.34 in the latest trading session, marking a -2.48% move from the prior day. The stock's change was less than the S&P 500's daily gain of 0.42%. Meanwhile, the Dow experienced a rise of 0.29%, and the technology-dominated Nasdaq saw an increase of 0.29%.

Shares of the company have appreciated by 15.61% over the course of the past month, outperforming the Computer and Technology sector's gain of 0.85%, and the S&P 500's gain of 2.2%.

The investment community will be closely monitoring the performance of Reddit Inc. in its forthcoming earnings report. The company is scheduled to release its earnings on July 30, 2026. In that report, analysts expect Reddit Inc. to post earnings of $0.99 per share. This would mark year-over-year growth of 120%. Meanwhile, our latest consensus estimate is calling for revenue of $746.89 million, up 49.49% from the prior-year quarter.

For the entire fiscal year, the Zacks Consensus Estimates are projecting earnings of $4.83 per share and a revenue of $3.25 billion, representing changes of +84.35% and +47.64%, respectively, from the prior year.

Additionally, investors should keep an eye on any recent revisions to analyst forecasts for Reddit Inc. Such recent modifications usually signify the changing landscape of near-term business trends. As a result, we can interpret positive estimate revisions as a good sign for the business outlook.

Our research suggests that these changes in estimates have a direct relationship with upcoming stock price performance. We developed the Zacks Rank to capitalize on this phenomenon. Our system takes these estimate changes into account and delivers a clear, actionable rating model.

The Zacks Rank system, spanning from #1 (Strong Buy) to #5 (Strong Sell), boasts an impressive track record of outperformance, audited externally, with #1 ranked stocks yielding an average annual return of +25% since 1988. Within the past 30 days, our consensus EPS projection remained stagnant. Reddit Inc. is holding a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy) right now.

Investors should also note Reddit Inc.'s current valuation metrics, including its Forward P/E ratio of 41.45. Its industry sports an average Forward P/E of 19.73, so one might conclude that Reddit Inc. is trading at a premium comparatively.

The Internet - Software industry is part of the Computer and Technology sector. This group has a Zacks Industry Rank of 92, putting it in the top 38% of all 250+ industries.

The Zacks Industry Rank evaluates the power of our distinct industry groups by determining the average Zacks Rank of the individual stocks forming the groups. Our research shows that the top 50% rated industries outperform the bottom half by a factor of 2 to 1.

title: AAOI Soared 251%, But PSI Quietly Doubled Your Money Too
detail:
AAOI Soared 251%, But PSI Quietly Doubled Your Money Too
Michael Williams
Fri, July 10, 2026 at 2:17 PM EDT 5 min read
AAOI
RDDT
PSI
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure
Quick Read

AAOI surged 250% year-to-date, but PSI, a semiconductor ETF riding the same AI wave, delivered 102%, nearly doubling a $10,000 stake.

AAOI dropped 25% in a single month while Reddit sentiment crashed from bullish to bearish in 72 hours, the part nobody screenshots.

Many financial professionals are salespeople paid on what they push, not whether you end up wealthier. A fiduciary is the opposite. The SEC legally requires them to put your interests first. Advisor.com's free matching tool pairs you with vetted fiduciaries from major national firms, all in under three minutes. See who you match with today.

You saw the chart. Applied Optoelectronics (NASDAQ:AAOI) went vertical this year, and your feed will not shut up about it. The screenshots, the rocket emojis, the "full port" posts. And you didn't buy a single share.

Quality Stock Arts / Shutterstock.com

The optical transceiver maker is up 250.57% year to date, riding the AI datacenter buildout that needs faster and faster fiber to move data between GPUs. That is the kind of return that ruins your week when you missed it.

Here is the twist: you didn't miss it. Not really.

The Number That Kills the FOMO

Over the same window, from December 31, 2025 through July 9, 2026, the Invesco Semiconductors ETF (NYSEARCA:PSI) returned 102.24%. A $10,000 stake at the start of the year sat at roughly $20,220 on July 9.

That is a serious return. A chip basket that more than doubled in a little over six months while you slept, worked, and refreshed your brokerage app in peace.

PSI is a basket of U.S.-listed semiconductor names, an index-tracking fund from Invesco that spreads exposure across the sector for an expense ratio of roughly 0.56%. You paid a rounding error to own the theme.

Are You Ready To Retire, Or Years Behind?

Most Americans suspect they're behind on retirement and never find out. Advisor.com's free matching tool pairs you in about three minutes with a vetted fiduciary advisor who can help you with investing, taxes, retirement, estate planning, and more. No minimums. No sales call. Find out where you stand.

Same Wave, Different Surfboard

The force that lifted Applied Optoelectronics is the broader AI capex wave: hyperscalers pouring money into datacenters, GPUs needing high-speed interconnects, and optical transceivers stepping up from 400G to 800G and 1.6 Tb products. AAOI's Q1 2026 datacenter revenue more than doubled year over year to $81.4 million, and CEO Thompson Lin said the company "completed our first volume shipment of our 800G products to one of our large hyperscale customers in Q1."

title: Jefferies Believes RDDT’s Q2 Will Show Momentum – A Look At Why This Analyst Expects A 22% Upside For The Stock
detail:
Jefferies Believes RDDT’s Q2 Will Show Momentum – A Look At Why This Analyst Expects A 22% Upside For The Stock
Jefferies Believes RDDT’s Q2 Will Show Momentum – A Look At Why This Analyst Expects A 22% Upside For The Stock · Stocktwits
Ahmed Farhath
Fri, July 10, 2026 at 1:29 PM EDT 2 min read
JEF
RDDT
SNAP
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

The company said in Q1 that it would bolster its advertising capabilities to turn human attention into a bigger revenue stream.

Platforms losing share to Reddit were LinkedIn in business-to-business advertising, Pinterest, Snapchat, and X, according to the agencies who spoke to Jefferies, Investing.com reported.

Analysts expect the company to generate $731 million in revenue and $1.45 per share in adjusted profit for Q2.

Reddit (RDDT) attracted significant investor attention on Friday after Jefferies issued positive commentary on the company following conversations with advertising agencies ahead of the social media platform's second-quarter earnings, which are expected at the end of this month.

The firm expects Reddit to show strong momentum in the second quarter (Q2), according to a report from Investing.com, as the company has frequently been cited by ad firms for taking market share from social media budgets.

See what 10M+ investors are talking about. Get the Stocktwits Daily Rip for what retail is watching right now, free to your inbox

The company is scheduled to report earnings on July 30. Analysts expect the company to generate $731 million in revenue and $1.45 per share in adjusted profit, according to data from Koyfin.

Reddit Gaining Social Share Through Strong Contextual Targeting

The report said advertising agencies noted accelerated digital ad growth in Q2, with Reddit gaining social share through strong contextual targeting, high-intent audience, and incremental reach.

Platforms losing share to Reddit were LinkedIn in business-to-business advertising, Pinterest, Snapchat, and X, according to the agencies who spoke to Jefferies. Moreover, Reddit's investments in automation tools benefited smaller advertisers and drove adoption among small and medium-sized businesses.

Jefferies maintained its 'Buy' rating on RDDT with a $250 price target, implying roughly 22% upside potential from the stock's current level.

Reddit Executing On Advertising Strategy

The analyst commentary affirms that the company is executing on its ad strategy, the details of which it disclosed in its first quarter. At that time, the company said it would further bolster its advertising capabilities to turn human attention into a bigger revenue stream, after reporting 74% growth in ad revenue for Q1.

"We're executing this strategy across three areas: number one, scaling automation through our ads platform in Reddit Max; number two, delivering advertiser value across all objectives; number three, expanding the Reddit for Business ecosystem," Chief Operating Officer Jennifer Wong said at the time.

One Momentum Stock to Buy:

Why Do We Love AJG?

Annual revenue growth of 19.1% over the last two years was superb and indicates its market share increased during this cycle

Unparalleled revenue scale of $14.95 billion gives it an edge in distribution

AJG is a free cash flow machine with the flexibility to invest in growth initiatives or return capital to shareholders

title: Reddit (RDDT) Stock Sees Modest Fair Value Cut As Analysts Weigh Growth And Volatility
detail:
Reddit (RDDT) Stock Sees Modest Fair Value Cut As Analysts Weigh Growth And Volatility
Bailey Pemberton
Thu, July 9, 2026 at 9:22 PM EDT 3 min read
RDDT
Trade Reddit on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Find your next quality investment with Simply Wall St's easy and powerful screener, trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Reddit's updated fair value estimate has shifted from US$232.05 to US$224.92, giving investors a fresh reference point for how analysts are framing the stock. This adjustment sits alongside commentary that highlights earnings growth potential, Reddit's differentiated community model and ongoing volatility linked to factors such as insider selling and index inclusion debates. Read on to see how these elements fit together and how to follow the evolving narrative around Reddit over time.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value Reddit.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying
🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Several firms, including Wells Fargo, Goldman Sachs, BofA, Piper Sandler and Loop Capital, have raised their Reddit price targets in recent months, signaling that many on the Street see room for the stock to support higher valuation assumptions.

Loop Capital points to Reddit's topline growing over 70% for each of the trailing four quarters and highlights multiple potential growth drivers, while still viewing the current P/E multiple, based on expected 2027 earnings, as appealing.

Wells Fargo cites strong ad checks in Q2 and revenue that is 5% higher, along with expectations for Q3 revenue guidance slightly ahead of consensus, as key supports for its updated US$187 target and Equal Weight rating.

Wells Fargo is also "growing more constructive" on Reddit's content licensing renewals, suggesting that higher annual revenue from deals with Alphabet and OpenAI could be meaningful for the company's longer term monetisation profile.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Wells Fargo flags that third party data points to tepid user growth, with trimmed estimates for Q2 U.S. daily average user net adds, and argues that the Alphabet and OpenAI licensing deals create a significant overhang on Reddit's enterprise value.

Several houses, including Raymond James, Citizens and Rothschild & Co Redburn, have reduced their Reddit price targets, and Phillip Securities recently downgraded the stock, underscoring concerns around user growth pressure, valuation and execution risk.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!

title: My old job had a pension and they offered me to cash out for $24k or get $100 per month for life — which should I choose?
detail:
My old job had a pension and they offered me to cash out for $24k or get $100 per month for life — which should I choose?
Maurie Backman
Thu, July 9, 2026 at 9:01 PM EDT 6 min read
RDDT
Trade Reddit on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

When I hear about people who are eligible for a pension from an employer, I tend to feel a little jealous. Aside from Social Security, any money I have available in retirement is money I will probably have to save myself.

But the reality is that private sector companies have largely abandoned pensions in favor of shifting the savings burden onto employees. If you are lucky, you might have a 401(k) match thrown into the mix. I am self-employed, so I get none of that. Then again, I do get to set my own hours and work from the beach if I so choose, so there is that.

corolanty from Getty Images

Meanwhile, I recently came across a Reddit post where the author faces an interesting situation. They are entitled to a pension from a former employer and have two options: take about $100 per month for the rest of their life, or cash out the pension at around $24,000. It is not an easy decision. But there is a fairly straightforward way to arrive at a smart choice.

It all comes down to your break-even age

The decision to cash out a pension versus take a monthly payout should hinge largely on how long you think you will live. To figure that out, you need to calculate your break-even age. The math is simple. The poster could get $100 a month indefinitely or settle for $24,000 up front. Their break-even age is the age at which they receive the lump sum, plus 20 years. If they are eligible for the lump sum at 65, then at age 85 they should end up with the same cumulative amount whether they took the $24,000 in full or accepted $100 a month.

_________________________________

What's Your Number...?

Here's a question most people 5y from retirement can't answer: at your current savings rate, how much do you need, and how long will it actually last? A good advisor can put a date on that in a single meeting. SmartAsset's free quiz matches you with up to three fiduciary advisors serving your area, so you can get YOUR retirement number now (sponsor)

__________________________________________

The 2026 rate environment matters here. Federal law requires pensions to use Minimum Present Value Segment Rates to calculate payouts, and these rates carry an inverse relationship with the lump sum value: higher rates produce smaller lump sums. As a general rule, a 1% increase in segment rates reduces a lump sum value by roughly 10%, which means lump sum offers today remain significantly smaller than they were during the near-zero-rate environment of 2020 and 2021. It is also worth noting that Social Security carries a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026, while most private pensions are fixed. That $100 a month buys something real today, but its purchasing power will quietly erode over time.

Story Continues

title: Reddit to Announce Second Quarter Results on Thursday, July 30, 2026
detail:
This is a paid press release. Contact the press release distributor directly with any inquiries.
Reddit to Announce Second Quarter Results on Thursday, July 30, 2026
Business Wire
Thu, July 9, 2026 at 4:10 PM EDT 1 min read
RDDT
Trade Reddit on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

SAN FRANCISCO, July 09, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Reddit, Inc. (NYSE: RDDT), announced today that the company's second quarter 2026 financial results will be released after market close on Thursday, July 30, 2026.

Reddit will host a conference call to discuss its results at 1:30 p.m. PT / 4:30 p.m. ET the same day.

The live webcast of the conference call and related earnings materials can be accessed at Reddit's Investor Relations website at investor.redditinc.com and investor relations subreddit r/RDDT. A replay of the webcast will also be available following the call at the same websites.

Reddit will solicit questions from the community at r/RDDT on Thursday, July 30, 2026, and post responses following the earnings call at Reddit's Investor Relations website at investor.redditinc.com and investor relations subreddit r/RDDT.

About Reddit, Inc.

Reddit is a community of communities. Built on shared interests and passions, it is home to the most open and authentic conversations online. Every day, millions of people post, vote, comment, and search for answers across nearly every topic imaginable, and brands build trusted relationships with their audiences. With 25+ billion posts and comments and more than 126 million daily active uniques, Reddit is one of the internet's largest sources of information. Learn more at www.redditinc.com. The Reddit app is available on the App Store and Google Play.
